messages.content: If activeCell.Column = 11 And activeCell.Value <> "" Then how will I adjust this  using Offset, so that the cell just before the activeCell.Value is <> ""

You can adjust the code using Offset to check the value of the cell just before the activeCell. You can use the following code:

```
If ActiveCell.Column = 11 And ActiveCell.Offset(0, -1).Value <> "" Then
    'Your code here
End If
```

This code checks if the active cell is in column 11 and also checks the value of the cell just before using the Offset property with (-1) as the second argument. If the value is not empty, then the code inside the `If` statement will get executed.
The following code used to work. If I entered a value into Column 11, it would copy the range specified and past it into an text document.

You can modify the following code to achieve the desired outcome:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)

    If Target.Column = 11 And Target.Value <> "" Then
        
        'Copy the range specified and assign it to a variable
        Dim rng As Range
        Set rng = Range("A1:C5")
        rng.Copy
        
        'Create a new text file and write the copied range to it
        Open "C:\temp\output.txt" For Output As #1
        Print #1, rng.Value
        Close #1
        
    End If

End Sub
```

In this code, when a value is entered in column 11, the specified range (A1:C5) is copied to a variable named "rng". The code then opens a new text file named "output.txt" in the "C:\temp\" folder and writes the value of the copied range to it using the Print statement. Finally, the text file is closed. 

Note that you'll need to update the range to be copied and the file path to your specific requirements.

The following code used to work perfectly. If I entered a value into Column 11, it would copy the range specified and past it into an text document. Currently it opens the text document but does not paste the range into it. Can you detect what has gone wrong? Here is the full code: Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
Application.EnableEvents = True
If Target.Row > 1 Then
Cells.Interior.ColorIndex = 0 'Reset all cells to no color
Target.EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 6 'Highlight active row
End If
'Update the global variable in module2 with the address of the selected cell
selectedCellAddress = Target.Address

Dim Rng1 As Range
Dim RowNum As Integer

RowNum = activeCell.Row

Set Rng1 = Range("A" & RowNum & ":H" & RowNum)
         
    If Target.Cells.CountLarge > 1 Then Exit Sub 'only one cell at a time
    On Error Resume Next
    If Intersect(activeCell, Range("K1:K2001")) Is Nothing Then
    Exit Sub
    
    Else
    If activeCell.Column = 11 And activeCell.Offset(0, -1).Value <> "" Then
    Rng1.Copy
    
        ThisWorkbook.Sheets(activeCell.Value).Activate
        
Shell "notepad.exe", vbNormalFocus
SendKeys "^V"
SendKeys "^{HOME}"

    End If
End If
End Sub

The reason why the range is not being pasted into the text document is because the code is not actually pasting it into the file using SendKeys. 

To paste the copied range into the text document opened using Notepad, you would need to modify the code as follows:

```
If activeCell.Column = 11 And activeCell.Offset(0, -1).Value <> "" Then
    Rng1.Copy
    
    ThisWorkbook.Sheets(activeCell.Value).Activate
        
    'Open Notepad and wait for it to become active
    Shell "notepad.exe", vbNormalFocus
    Application.Wait Now + TimeValue("00:00:01")
    
    'Paste the copied range into Notepad
    SendKeys "^V"
    
    'Save and close the text document
    SendKeys "%f"
    SendKeys "a"
    SendKeys "output.txt"
    SendKeys "%s"
    SendKeys "%{F4}"
    
End If
```

This updated code first copies the specified range to the clipboard, activates the worksheet with the name specified in the active cell, and then opens Notepad using the Shell function. After waiting for 1 second for the Notepad window to become active, it pastes the copied range into Notepad using SendKeys "^V". 

Once the text is pasted, SendKeys saves the file with the name "output.txt" and then closes the text document using SendKeys "%{F4}".

Note that this approach of using SendKeys can be fragile and may not always work as intended, especially if there are other programs open on the user's computer or if the focus of their computer is not solely on Excel. Consider using other more robust methods such as automating Microsoft Word or creating and editing text files with VBA directly instead.

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
Application.EnableEvents = True
If Target.Row > 1 Then
Cells.Interior.ColorIndex = 0 'Reset all cells to no color
Target.EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 6 'Highlight active row
End If
'Update the global variable in module2 with the address of the selected cell
selectedCellAddress = Target.Address

Dim Rng1 As Range
Dim RowNum As Integer

RowNum = activeCell.Row

Set Rng1 = Range("A" & RowNum & ":H" & RowNum)
         
    If Target.Cells.CountLarge > 1 Then Exit Sub 'only one cell at a time
    On Error Resume Next
    If Intersect(activeCell, Range("K1:K2001")) Is Nothing Then
    Exit Sub
    
    Else
    If activeCell.Column = 11 And activeCell.Offset(0, -1).Value <> "" Then
    Rng1.Copy
    
        'ThisWorkbook.Sheets(activeCell.Value).Activate
        
Shell "notepad.exe", vbNormalFocus
Application.Wait Now + TimeValue("00:00:01")
SendKeys "^V"
SendKeys "^{HOME}"

Application.Wait Now + TimeValue("00:00:02")
ThisWorkbook.Sheets(activeCell.Value).Activate

    End If
End If
End Sub

In the code above, I do not understand what the following line does;

    If Intersect(activeCell, Range("K1:K2001")) Is Nothing Then

Can you expalin

The line of code checks if the selected cell, referred to as `activeCell`, is located within the range "K1:K2001". `Intersect` is a function that determines if two ranges overlap. Here, it is being used to check if `activeCell` intersects with the specified range. If `activeCell` is not within the range, the code exits the procedure using `Exit Sub`. 

This check is included to ensure that the code only runs when a cell is selected within the specified range and avoids unwanted interference with cells outside of that range.

Originally the line of code read, If Intersect(activeCell, Range(“K1:J2001”)) Is Nothing Then, I recently changed it to If Intersect(activeCell, Range(“K1:K2001”)) Is Nothing Then. Would you have any thoughts why the intersect was K1:J2001 and explain what happens when an intersect is for two columns and if my intersect on one column makes sense.

The range "K1:J2001" does not make sense as it refers to columns K through J. The first letter in the range (K) specifies the column of the first cell, and the last letter in the range (J) specifies the column of the last cell. Because J comes before K in the alphabet, this range includes cells in columns J, I, H, and G, rather than in the intended columns K, L, M, N, O, P, Q, and R.

As for the second question, it is possible to specify a range that includes multiple columns, such as "K1:M2001", in which case the code would check for an intersection between the selected cell and the cells in all three columns (K, L, and M). It is possible to use a range that includes only one column, such as "K1:K2001", if that is the only column in which you want the code to be activated.

In short, the range should include all the cells that the code is intended to target, and should not include any unintended cells.

With respect to my earlier enquiry, the range is now being copied and pasted into a text document. However, it is pasting the data into a single line. How can I get it to paste each cell value into a seperate line in the text document

To paste each cell value into a separate line in the text document, you can use the `vbCrLf` character to add a new line after each value. 

Replace this line:

```
SendKeys "^V"
```

with these lines:

```
For Each cell In Rng1
    SendKeys cell.Value
    SendKeys vbCrLf
Next cell
```

This code loops through each cell in `Rng1`, pasting the cell value and adding a new line using `vbCrLf` after each value. This will result in each cell value being on a separate line in the text document.

Note: As I mentioned earlier, the use of SendKeys can be fragile and may not always work as intended, so you may wish to consider more robust methods such as automating Microsoft Word, Excel, or creating and editing text files with VBA directly instead.

For the sepecific Excel sheet, the workbook calculation is turned of.
I enter values in columns 1 to 8 columns and column 9 is time stamped with a vba code.
Column 10 has the following formula; =IF($A74<>"",VLOOKUP($A74,LIST!$A$2:$C$54,3,FALSE),"").... but this does not update unless I force calculation of the sheet.

Another VBA code needs to look at the value of column 10 to for it to perform the desired action.

The VBA timestamp does not appear to be affected by the sheet being set to manual calculation. Shouuld I use a VBA to fill in values I need for Column 10? What solutions or suggestions do ou recommend.

Since you mentioned that the workbook calculation is turned off, the formula in column 10 will not update automatically. This could lead to issues when the value in column 1 changes and needs to trigger the code that looks at the value of column 10.

One possible solution is to use VBA to fill in the values in column 10. This way, you can ensure that the column is always up-to-date and the code that references it will work correctly.

You can use the following code to fill in column 10 with the VLOOKUP results:

```
Sub UpdateColumn10()
    Dim lastRow As Long
    With Worksheets("Sheet1")
        'Find the last row with data
        lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
        
        'Fill in column 10 with the VLOOKUP results
        .Range("J2:J" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = _
            "=IF(RC[-9]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-9],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"
    End With
End Sub
```

This code fills in column 10 (cell J2 to the last row with data) with the VLOOKUP results based on the value in column 1. The formula is entered using the R1C1 notation, which allows you to refer to columns and rows relative to the cell being updated. 

You can call this code every time there is a change to column 1 using the following code in the worksheet module:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Not Application.CalculationState = xlDone Then Exit Sub
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        UpdateColumn10
        Application.EnableEvents = True
    End If
End Sub
```

This code checks if the workbook calculation is done (`Application.CalculationState = xlDone`), and whether the change occurred in column 1 (`Target.Column = 1`). If so, it temporarily disables events (`Application.EnableEvents = False`), calls the `UpdateColumn10` subroutine to fill in column 10, and then re-enables events (`Application.EnableEvents = True`). 

This approach ensures that column 10 is always up-to-date and avoids potential issues caused by the formula not updating automatically.

Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
'Application.EnableEvents = True
If Not Application.CalculationState = xlDone Then Exit Sub
If Target.Column = 1 Then
Application.EnableEvents = False
Call UpdateColumn11
Application.EnableEvents = True
End If
    TimeStampSimple Target, "G2", "J", "mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss", False
End Sub

Sub UpdateColumn11()
Application.EnableEvents = True
Dim lastRow As Long
With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
' Find the last row with data
lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row

' Fill in column 11 with the VLOOKUP results
.Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = _
"=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"
End With
'Application.EnableEvents = False
End Sub. The code is not updating Column 11.

One issue with the code may be that `Application.EnableEvents = True` is enabled at the beginning of the `UpdateColumn11` subroutine. This can cause the `Worksheet_Change` event to trigger again, which can lead to an infinite loop.

Also, since `Application.EnableEvents` is set to `False` in the `Worksheet_Change` event, it is not necessary to set it again in the `UpdateColumn11` subroutine.

To fix this issue, you can remove the line `Application.EnableEvents = True` from the beginning of the `UpdateColumn11` subroutine, and use `Application.EnableEvents = False` at the beginning of the `Worksheet_Change` event only.

Try modifying the code as follows:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Not Application.CalculationState = xlDone Then Exit Sub
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        Call UpdateColumn11
        Application.EnableEvents = True
    End If
    TimeStampSimple Target, "G2", "J", "mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss", False
End Sub

Sub UpdateColumn11()
    Dim lastRow As Long
    With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
        ' Find the last row with data
        lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row

        ' Fill in column 11 with the VLOOKUP results
        .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = _
            "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"
    End With
End Sub
```

This code will only enable events once the `UpdateColumn11` subroutine has been called and executed, avoiding potential infinite loops.

I have followed your recommendation and used the code below. However, it is still not updating column 11 when I enter values in column 1. Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
If Not Application.CalculationState = xlDone Then Exit Sub
If Target.Column = 1 Then
Application.EnableEvents = False
Call UpdateColumn11
Application.EnableEvents = True

    TimeStampSimple Target, "G2", "J", "mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss", False
End Sub

' Fill in column 11 with the VLOOKUP results
.Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = _

End With

If the code is still not updating column 11, there could be a few reasons. Here are some potential solutions:

1. Confirm that the formula in column 10 (column K) is updating correctly when you enter values in column 1. You can do this by manually updating the formula in one row and confirming that the result is correct. If the formula is not correct, it may be preventing the VLOOKUP in column 11 from updating correctly.

2. Change the formula in column 11 (column L) to a simpler formula, such as `=A2`, and confirm that the formula updates correctly when you enter values in column 1. This will help you confirm if the issue is with the VLOOKUP function or with the code.

3. Add some debug statements to the code to confirm that the `UpdateColumn11` subroutine is being called, and that there are no errors in the code. For example, you can add the following line at the beginning of the `UpdateColumn11` subroutine:

```
Debug.Print "UpdateColumn11 called"
```

This will print a message in the Immediate Window each time the subroutine is called. You can also add a breakpoint to the first line of the `Worksheet_Change` event and step through the code to confirm that the VLOOKUP formula is being applied correctly.

4. Confirm that the sheet name, range, and column numbers in the VLOOKUP formula are correct. Check that the sheet name "LIST" is spelled correctly and that the range "A2:C54" covers the correct cells. Also confirm that the third argument (column index number) is set to 3, which would return the value in column C.

5. If none of these solutions work, consider sharing a sample file with me so I can take a closer look.
